html:
Georg von Derfflinger, born March 20, 1606, and died March 14, 1695, was an Austrian imperial baron and military figure.

Derfflinger was a Protestant peasant's son from Austria. During the Thirty Years' War, he served in the Swedish army and became known as one of the most capable cavalry commanders. After Johan Banér's death in 1641, Derfflinger acted as the representative of the cavalry officers in their negotiations with representatives of the Swedish government. In 1643, he was promoted to major general but retired to his estates in Gusow after the peace treaty in 1648. In 1654, he entered service with Brandenburg and played a distinguished role in the war until 1660, including at the Battle of Warsaw, and rose to the rank of field marshal. After the peace, he returned to his estates but remained in service and was promoted to field marshal in 1670. In recognition of his participation in the war against France in 1672, Derfflinger was made a hereditary baron in 1674. In the subsequent war against Sweden, Derfflinger surprised the Swedes at Rathenow and contributed significantly to the victory at the Battle of Fehrbellin in 1675. In 1676, he successfully led the siege of Stettin, and in 1678, he captured Rügen and Stralsund. In 1679, he repelled the Swedish attack on Prussia but retired that same year after the peace treaty. Derfflinger was considered a daring and skilled cavalry general and one of the first organizers of the Brandenburg-Prussian army.

== Sources == Svensk uppslagsbok (Swedish Encyclopedia). Malmö, 1931.

=== Notes ===

== Further Reading == G. Jacobson: Georg von Derfflinger in Svenskt biografiskt lexikon (1945)

How certain are we about this?

Figures from the era of great powers are well-documented in archives and contemporary publications. Most information about who they were and what they did is verified; the uncertainty lies in interpretation and motives.
tldr: Georg von Derfflinger was a skilled military leader who served both Sweden and Brandenburg-Prussia during the 17th century. He became known for his successes in several major wars and achieved high ranks within the army.
lattlast:
Georg von Derfflinger was born in Austria in 1606. He grew up as a farmer's son, but became a highly skilled officer in the cavalry, which is the branch of the military that uses horses. During the Thirty Years' War, he worked for Sweden and rose to the rank of major general after demonstrating great skill in combat.

After the peace treaty was signed in 1648, he switched sides and began working for Brandenburg-Prussia, a region that is now part of Germany. There, he quickly advanced in rank and became a field marshal, which is a very high military title. He participated in several important battles and led successful sieges of cities.

For his contributions in the wars against France and Sweden, he was awarded the title of Imperial Baron, a high noble title that conferred status and power. He is described as a courageous general and a person who helped build up the Prussian army during the 17th century.
